data.title: Arena Group GAAP EPS of $0.08, revenue of $31.8M
data.description:

  • Financial Guidance for Q2 2025: Revenue of around $40-$45M.

data.title: Pentagon prepares framework for ‘Golden Dome’ defense project amid concerns
data.description:
The Pentagon completed an initial blueprint for the proposed “Golden Dome” air and missile defense system, according to the Department of Defense, as questions continue to surface regarding the feasibility and cost of the initiative promoted by the Trump administration.

The defense plan follows a January 27 executive order from President Donald Trump that directed the Pentagon to explore comprehensive options to enhance the nation’s missile defense capabilities.

In a statement to Bloomberg News, spokesperson Sean Parnell said the department enlisted top defense experts and engineers to assess both existing U.S. missile defense systems and emerging technologies. The objective, he added, is to rapidly build a “reliable umbrella of protection” for the United States.

Parnell noted that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and senior military officials have briefed the president on potential strategies and anticipate providing a formal update on the project's direction in the near future.

During a White House press event on Monday -- primarily focused on a separate executive order to lower prescription drug costs -- an image promoting the Golden Dome concept was displayed. President Trump claimed the expected savings from the healthcare initiative would offset the defense system’s cost, though no financial specifics were disclosed.

Prior to the Pentagon’s latest comments, neither the Department of Defense nor the White House had shared technical specifications, development timelines or budget projections for the Golden Dome effort. That lack of transparency has raised concerns among lawmakers involved in authorizing defense funding.

An analysis by the Congressional Budget Office highlights the financial stakes, estimating that the most controversial portion of the program -- space-based interceptors -- could cost anywhere between $161 billion and $542 billion over the next two decades. The final cost will depend on the scale of deployment and associated launch expenses, according to a CBO report prepared for a Senate Armed Services subcommittee.

Meanwhile, the Defense Intelligence Agency released an updated threat assessment identifying missile threats the system is designed to counter. Lieutenant General Jeffrey Kruse, DIA director, told lawmakers in written testimony that the agency is supplying essential intelligence to guide the system’s design and implementation.

The Golden Dome remains in its early stages, but its scope and cost are likely to remain key points of debate as the administration moves forward with the initiative.

Several U.S. companies have expressed interest in contributing to the development of the Golden Dome missile defense system.

Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) publicly announced its readiness to support the Golden Dome project. The company is evaluating various assets, including its command-and-control networks, F-35 stealth fighter and Sentinel A4 radar, to support the initiative. Lockheed Martin's (NYSE:LMT) technology, like the TPY-6 radar and the PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement, has shown success in recent tests.

SpaceX (SPACE), led by Elon Musk, has emerged as a frontrunner to assist in developing the Golden Dome missile defense system. The company is collaborating with data analytics firm Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) and defense company Anduril to build crucial components of the system. Their plan includes launching 400 to more than 1,000 satellites for tracking, alongside a separate fleet of 200 attack satellites potentially armed with missiles or lasers. However, SpaceX (SPACE) is expected to focus solely on satellite tracking, not weaponization.

BlueHalo, the defense technology company acquired by AV (NASDAQ:AVAV), formerly known as AeroVironment, sees an opportunity for the Golden Dome project to encompass more than missile defense. The company advocates for integrating counter-drone capabilities and electronic warfare into the system, emphasizing the need for a comprehensive approach to modern threats.

RTX (NYSE:RTX), Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) and General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) also are among the traditional defense contractors expressing interest in the Golden Dome initiative.

data.title: Comstock appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Comstock (NYSE:LODE) has approved the appointment of Judd B. Merrill, as chief financial officer of the company and president of LODE’s wholly-owned mining subsidiary, Comstock Mining.
  • Merrill will assume his new role starting May 19, 2025.
  • Merrill, most recently served as CFO of Aqua Metals (AQMS).
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Microsoft remains attractive for long-term investors due to Azure, AI strategy: Evercore
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) remains an attractive long-term company for investors as the software giant's artificial intelligence strategy is working, and its Azure segment continues to demonstrate impressive growth quarter after quarter, according to Evercore ISI.

"The bottom line is that with MSFT trading at 29x CY26 GAAP EPS, we continue to believe that the shares offer an attractive risk/reward for long-term investors," said Evercore analysts, led by Kirk Materne, in a Thursday investor note.

After dropping to a 17-month low in early April, shares have surged over the past month and are approaching the all-time high set last summer.

Evercore rates the stock at Outperform with a $500 price target.

"We estimate AI-consumption on Azure passed a $14bn run-rate and M365 Copilot approached a $2.5bn run-rate," Materne noted. "However, what's more important, is that we are still in the very early days of enterprise AI adoption and the AI business should remain a meaningful catalyst when looking ahead to FY26/FY27."

"As AI capacity continues to come online and the non-AI business stabilizes, we believe that Azure should remain a durable 30%+ revenue grower for the next several quarters – even when assuming some normalization of growth in the non-AI part of Azure," Materne added.

Evercore is holding its annual Microsoft 360 Day next Tuesday.

data.title: Druckenmiller's Duquesne adds Docusign, CCC; exits Skechers, SLM, others in Q1
data.description:
Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office closed out its holdings in Skechers (SKX), SLM (SLM), and US Steel (X), and added new holdings in Docusign (DOCU), CCC Intelligent Solutions (CCCS), and EQT (EQT), among its trades in Q1 2025, according to its 13F filing.

The company acquired 1.07M shares of Docusign (DOCU), valued at ~$87.5M at March 31, 2025, ~5.96M shares of CCC Intelligent Solutions (CCCS) ($50.5M), and 859K shares of EQT Corp. (EQT) ($45.9M) during the quarter.

The firm increased stakes in: Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) to 377K ($83.6M) from 18.1K, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSMC) to ~599K shares ($99.4M) from ~108K; and Insmed (INSM) to 1.37M shares ($104.4M) from 593K shares.

It reduced its holdings in: Coherent Corp. (COHR) to 2.22M shares ($144.3M) from 2.82M shares, Seagate (STX) to 219K shares ($18.6M) from 1.56M, and Natera (NTRA) to 3.40M shares ($481.1M) from 3.57M, according to the filing.

data.title: AIG announces redemption of notes due 2026
data.description:
Insurer American International Group (NYSE:AIG) announced on Thursday the redemption of all of its outstanding 3.900% notes due 2026.

The redemption price equals the sum of the principal amount of the notes, accrued and unpaid interest thereon, and a customary "make-whole" premium calculated in accordance with the indenture governing the notes.

As of May 15, $236.16M in aggregate principal amount of the notes were outstanding.

data.title: Skillsoft appoints CFO
data.description:
Skillsoft (NYSE:SKIL) announced on Thursday the appointment of Chief Transformation Officer John Frederick as CFO, effective immediately.

Frederick succeeds Rich Walker, who is stepping down to pursue other opportunities. Walker will serve in an advisory capacity until July 4.

Shares were +0.09% post market to $23.39.

data.title: Wall Street ends mixed, but the benchmark S&P 500 extends rally to four straight days
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday ended mixed, but its benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) extended a rally to four straight days and further solidified itself in positive territory for the year. 

There were some signs of buyer fatigue in today's session, and some lackluster economic data also pressured sentiment. However, a jump in defensive sectors such as utilities and consumer staples helped offset that. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.41% to close at 5,916.93 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed +0.65% to conclude at 42,322.75 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.18% to settle at 19,112.32 points.

"Today was a great day for everyone overweight value/dividend stocks. Lower-than-expected producer prices and a surprisingly strong Philadelphia Fed manufacturing survey confirmed that the economy is, in fact, not headed for stagflation," Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha. 

"Hence, it happens exactly what I expected, which is a move into the most attractive industries after the latest S&P 500 rally has pushed the valuation of most growth stocks back to unfavorable levels. If this continues, I expect the rotation from growth to value stocks to last," Nelissen added. 

Earlier, before the opening bell on Thursday, investors received a slew of economic indicators. First up was the April producer price index (PPI) report, with both headline and core producer prices unexpectedly declining on a M/M basis. 

At the same time, the U.S. Census Bureau said retail sales in April edged up +0.1% M/M, just above the consensus for a flat reading. Core retail sales also increased by a similar amount, but came in below the expected rise.    

Additionally, two measures of manufacturing activity continued to show weakness in the sector. 

Among Thursday's active stocks, Walmart (WMT) ended -0.5%. The U.S. retailing giant's management on an earnings conference call warned that tariffs could lead to higher prices for consumers. 

Offsetting some of Walmart's (WMT) weakness was Cisco (CSCO). Shares of the networking giant were +4.8% after its solid quarterly results and guidance.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 8 basis points to 4.45%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 9 basis points to 3.97%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.
